name: App Startup Optimization
description: Reduce cold start time by deferring initialization and using baseline profiles.
when_to_use: When cold start exceeds 500ms or users abandon before first frame.
trade_offs: Deferred init can cause runtime delays on first use of those features.
implementation_notes: Android: App Startup library + Baseline Profiles + R8 optimization. iOS: reduce didFinishLaunching work; pre-warm caches.
keywords: startup cold warm launch time baseline profile
references: Android App Startup; Apple Launch Guide

name: Memory Management & Leak Prevention
description: Monitor and reduce memory footprint to prevent OOM crashes and jank.
when_to_use: Always; especially on low-end devices with 2-3GB RAM.
trade_offs: Aggressive cleanup can cause re-creation overhead.
implementation_notes: Android: LeakCanary + weak references + lifecycle-aware cleanup. iOS: Instruments Allocations + weak/unowned. KMM: careful with captured lambdas.
keywords: memory leak oom leakcanary instruments allocation

name: Frame Rate & Rendering Performance
description: Maintain 60fps (16ms frame budget) or 120fps on high-refresh displays.
when_to_use: When UI shows jank; dropped frames; or animation stuttering.
trade_offs: Profiling overhead in debug builds can mask real performance characteristics.
implementation_notes: Android: GPU Profiler + Layout Inspector + recomposition counter. iOS: Core Animation instrument. Compose: avoid unnecessary recompositions.
keywords: frame rate fps jank rendering recomposition overdraw

name: Network Efficiency
description: Minimize data transfer; reduce round trips; and implement smart caching.
when_to_use: When app makes frequent API calls; loads media; or operates on metered connections.
trade_offs: Aggressive caching can show stale data; compression adds CPU overhead.
implementation_notes: HTTP caching (ETag/Last-Modified). Image: Coil/Glide (Android); Kingfisher (iOS). Batch API calls. gzip/brotli compression.
keywords: network caching http image coil glide kingfisher

name: Battery Optimization
description: Reduce background processing and wake locks to extend battery life.
when_to_use: When app performs background sync; location tracking; or periodic updates.
trade_offs: Less frequent updates mean less fresh data; must balance UX vs battery.
implementation_notes: Android: WorkManager (not AlarmManager). iOS: BGTaskScheduler. Avoid wake locks. Batch background work.
keywords: battery drain background wakelock workmanager doze
references: Android Battery Guide; Apple Energy Guide

name: Image Loading & Memory
description: Efficiently load; cache; decode; and display images at appropriate resolutions.
when_to_use: When app displays user-generated images; product photos; or media feeds.
trade_offs: Downsampling saves memory but can show blurry images if threshold is wrong.
implementation_notes: Android: Coil (Compose-first) or Glide. iOS: Kingfisher or Nuke. Always specify size constraints. WebP/AVIF format.
keywords: image loading cache coil glide kingfisher nuke webp

name: Compose Performance
description: Optimize Jetpack Compose recomposition to avoid unnecessary UI updates.
when_to_use: When using Jetpack Compose or Compose Multiplatform for UI.
trade_offs: Stability rules are non-obvious; immutable data classes can help.
implementation_notes: Use @Stable/@Immutable annotations. Avoid unstable lambdas in LazyColumn items. derivedStateOf for computed values. Use key() in lists.
keywords: compose recomposition stable immutable derivedstateof lazy

name: Code Shrinking & Obfuscation
description: Remove unused code and obfuscate to reduce APK/IPA size and protect IP.
when_to_use: When preparing release builds for production deployment.
trade_offs: Aggressive shrinking can remove needed code; thorough testing of release builds required.
implementation_notes: Android: R8 (ProGuard replacement) with keep rules. iOS: Bitcode + Swift symbol stripping. Both: tree shaking.
keywords: proguard r8 shrink obfuscate apk size strip

name: Local Database Performance
description: Optimize queries; indexing; and schema for fast local data operations.
when_to_use: When app uses Room/CoreData/SQLDelight with complex queries or large datasets.
trade_offs: Over-indexing increases write time and storage; analyze query plans.
implementation_notes: Index frequently queried columns. Use @Transaction for batch operations. Avoid main-thread queries. Enable WAL mode.
keywords: database room coredata sqldelight query index performance

name: Animation Performance
description: Maintain smooth animations without frame drops or visual artifacts.
when_to_use: When implementing custom transitions; shared element animations; or complex motion.
trade_offs: Complex animations can be CPU/GPU intensive; test on low-end devices.
implementation_notes: Android: Compose animation APIs with animateAsState. iOS: Core Animation with CATransaction. Keep under 300ms. Use hardware layers.
keywords: animation motion frame drop transition smooth performance

name: Startup Tracing & Metrics
description: Measure and monitor app startup time with structured traces and benchmarks.
when_to_use: When optimizing startup or setting up continuous performance monitoring.
trade_offs: Instrumentation overhead in debug; use release-mode benchmarks for accuracy.
implementation_notes: Android: Macrobenchmark + Perfetto traces. iOS: MetricKit + os_signpost. Firebase Performance Monitoring.
keywords: startup trace benchmark perfetto macrobenchmark metrickit

name: Lazy Initialization
description: Defer object creation until first use to reduce startup cost and memory.
when_to_use: When heavy objects (databases; analytics; SDKs) are not needed immediately.
trade_offs: First-use latency spike; thread safety considerations for lazy singletons.
implementation_notes: Kotlin: by lazy { }. Swift: lazy var. Android: App Startup InitializerProvider. Avoid lazy on hot paths.

name: Gradle Build Performance & Cleanup
description: Manage the Gradle Daemon and IDE caches to ensure clean and fast builds.
when_to_use: When experiencing inexplicable build errors or slow incremental builds.
trade_offs: Stopping daemon slows down next build; invalidating caches requires re-indexing.
implementation_notes: Run ./gradlew --stop to kill stale daemons. Use File > Invalidate Caches in Android Studio. Delete .gradle/ and build/ folders.
keywords: gradle daemon stop invalidate cache build cleanup
